title: Cash vs Digital Tracking
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can digital tracking reveal spending patterns you weren't aware of?
What balance between automated tracking and manual review works best for your lifestyle?

options.label: They disappear
options.reflection: Digital payments don't disappear - they leave electronic trails that can be tracked and analyzed.

options.label: Easy to forget about them
options.reflection: While it's easy to overlook small digital transactions, they're easier to track than cash spending.

options.label: They track themselves automatically
options.reflection: While digital payments create records, you still need to actively review them for financial awareness.

options.label: They leave records
options.reflection: Exactly! Digital payments create automatic records that help you monitor spending patterns.

stages.prompt: Which tracking method gives better spending insight?

options.label: Cash only - what's left in wallet
options.reflection: Cash tracking is limited and doesn't provide detailed spending analysis or patterns.

options.label: Just remember major purchases
options.reflection: Memory-based tracking misses small but significant daily expenses that add up over time.

options.label: Digital records with categorization
options.reflection: Perfect! Digital tracking with categories reveals spending patterns and helps identify areas for improvement.

options.label: Keep all physical receipts
options.reflection: Physical receipts are cumbersome to organize and analyze compared to digital records.

stages.prompt: How often should you review digital transaction records?

options.label: Never - the bank handles it
options.reflection: Passive banking doesn't provide the financial awareness needed for smart money management.

options.label: Weekly check-ins for awareness
options.reflection: Excellent! Weekly reviews build spending awareness and allow for timely course corrections.

options.label: Monthly review of statements
options.reflection: Good baseline! Monthly reviews help catch irregularities and track progress toward goals.

options.label: Daily micro-management
options.reflection: While thorough, daily tracking can become obsessive and time-consuming for most people.

stages.prompt: What's the biggest advantage of digital payment tracking?

options.label: Spending awareness and pattern recognition
options.reflection: Exactly! Tracking reveals unconscious spending habits and helps optimize your budget.

options.label: Convenience of not carrying cash
options.reflection: While convenient, this isn't the primary benefit of tracking digital payments.

options.label: Enhanced security features
options.reflection: Security is important but not the main advantage of actively tracking your digital payments.

options.label: Cashback and reward points
options.reflection: Rewards are nice but shouldn't be the primary reason for tracking your spending.

stages.prompt: Which tool is most effective for digital payment tracking?

options.label: Bank's basic online portal
options.reflection: Basic portals provide limited categorization and analysis capabilities.

options.label: Manual spreadsheet tracking
options.reflection: Manual tracking is time-intensive and prone to human error and inconsistency.

options.label: Trust your memory to remember spending
options.reflection: Human memory is unreliable for financial tracking and misses important spending patterns.

options.label: Dedicated budgeting apps with automation
options.reflection: Perfect! These apps automatically categorize spending and provide detailed insights.
